sources.name: TGSIM Foggy Bottom Trajectories
sources.license_note: Public data.gov dataset; check resource terms before redistribution.
sources.coordinate_quality: meter-scale coordinates; published conversion factor from pixels to meters
sources.scene_geometry: aerial reference image plus 49 polygon boundaries for road/crosswalk/intersection regions
sources.t100_readiness: strong: 0.1s records over a 2-hour window, enough for many t+100 windows
sources.size_note: main CSV around 350 MB plus reference image and region annotations
sources.why_it_matters: Best immediate upgrade for physical world modeling because it includes acceleration, object size, meters, and scene polygons.
sources.loader_status: stage3 adapter planned; no auto-download in this run

sources.name: Stanford Drone Dataset
sources.license_note: Creative Commons BY-NC-SA 3.0 on official page.
sources.coordinate_quality: image-space trajectories; scene-specific scale/homography must be estimated or supplied
sources.scene_geometry: top-view videos/reference frames; no full metric scene graph by default
sources.t100_readiness: medium-strong: long videos, but metric evaluation needs calibration
sources.size_note: official Stanford Campus download is large, about 69 GB
sources.why_it_matters: Large top-view campus interactions with multiple agent classes; good for social navigation and mixed-agent priors.
sources.loader_status: metadata/catalog only; avoid automatic 69 GB download

sources.name: OpenTraj Dataset Toolkit
sources.license_note: MIT toolkit; individual dataset licenses vary.
sources.coordinate_quality: mixed; includes world-2D datasets such as ETH, inD, DUT, VRU, and image-space datasets
sources.scene_geometry: mixed; some datasets include maps or context, many do not
sources.t100_readiness: medium: unified loaders help, but every source needs horizon/scene validation
sources.why_it_matters: Fastest way to compare multiple pedestrian trajectory datasets under one API.
sources.loader_status: adapter planned through optional external OpenTraj install

sources.name: ETH / UCY Pedestrian Trajectories
sources.license_note: Academic benchmark; verify each archive before redistribution.
sources.coordinate_quality: commonly used world/pixel pedestrian benchmark coordinates
sources.scene_geometry: limited; homographies often available through benchmark preprocessing
sources.t100_readiness: medium: good benchmark, but trajectories are shorter and less scene-rich
sources.why_it_matters: Canonical social trajectory benchmark for sanity-checking learned dynamics.

sources.name: TrajNet++
sources.t100_readiness: medium: interaction benchmark, but standard horizons may differ from t+100
sources.why_it_matters: Useful for stress-testing social interaction and collision-aware forecasting.

sources.name: OpenDD Roundabout Drone Dataset
sources.coordinate_quality: trajectory database with HD map information
sources.scene_geometry: HD map, shapefiles, geo-referenced drone images
sources.t100_readiness: strong for mixed traffic; pedestrian-only subset must be checked
sources.size_note: large multi-part dataset, 62+ hours according to official page
sources.why_it_matters: Excellent for map-aware constraints and mixed-agent physical interaction.
sources.loader_status: later-stage adapter; license restricts derived redistribution

sources.name: inD Intersection Drone Dataset
sources.t100_readiness: strong for mixed-agent intersections; pedestrian subset can evaluate long windows
sources.why_it_matters: Good mixed-agent interaction data but less immediately convenient than public TGSIM.
